## [00:00 - 03:00]

Mindset, the little known secrets of growth. Now, bear with me if you're thinking what's Mindset doing in the Facebook Ads course, but let me tell you something. If you don't get your Mindset right for testing, for scaling and knowing where your limits are in terms of risk and reward, you will lose a lot of money very quickly with Facebook Ads. If you don't believe Mindset is that important, then go ahead and do your testing, do your scaling, but really I really want you to bear in mind some of the points in this video and things you need to watch out for in order to be as successful as you can be. You know, wouldn't you want to minimize your losses? So let's talk about one of the kind of core principles I think has helped me to scale and understand how Facebook Ads work. Now go back to 2008 around that time, I started playing around with or in exchange trading currency pairs, day trading, etc. So I was buying and selling on the market. I will caveat that this was all in a test account. I never managed to go live, make too many losses in a test account to actually be able to take it live and make money from it, but the six to nine months I spent playing around with it was a great learning place to actually understand how the real world works in terms of buying and selling and auctions and about the kind of speculation and unpredictability of how things work in a marketplace. So if you look at the graph here on the right hand side, it's a typical graph that you'd be looking at when you're doing day trading. So the general line dictates where the final price ends up, but you can also see with the green and the red candle bars where the price went in in a given period. So for example these are days and you can see the highs and lows and whether the day ended up in a profit or a loss based on gains or losses. So a green means that it started off low and then it ended high, the red means that it started off high and then actually ended low. So you can kind of trace that pattern all the way through, but one of the key things that you learn with day trading is what your stop losses and when you should be ready to enter or in our case with Facebook ads ready to scale up. Get those two things right and you will minimize your losses and you'll maximize your profits as you're scaling up your ads. To give you an example, if you are scaling up 20% a day or duplicating ad sets, whichever strategy you choose to use knowing when things go wrong and when to cut and when to minimize your losses makes such a difference in terms of being able to scale out confidently as well because you'll know if you're running a Facebook ads campaign things go wrong you lose confidence quite quickly as well and being able to spot the trends in your data and know when and how to react is so

## [03:00 - 06:01]

crucial as well. And as with any kind of marketplace there are forces outside of your control which will have an impact on your day-to-day analysis and your day-to-day scaling of the account as well. So it may be that a big brand has put a lot of money into targeting your core audience and your costs rise and maybe your conversion rate goes down because a whole batch of competitors have come in or there's some other factors within Facebook that you haven't taken into account. So as much as we talk about campaign structure and funnels and optimizations and things like that there is also a lot outside of your control and this is why you need to be monitoring your account on a daily basis for me when I'm spending you know five figures a day I'm monitoring it multiple times of the day you need to be keeping an eye on your metrics and know when and how to react as well. So I want to talk about the risk and reward mindset so this is about how much are you willing to risk and how fast are you willing to scale up. If we look at this 2x2 matrix here what I've done is to put together from left to right the bid increase velocity so how quickly are you increasing your your bids your budgets etc and from bottom to top what kind of reward you are likely to expect. So if we look here in the bottom left we have the low risk quadrant so slow scale slow reward so let's say you're spending $10 a day you go up to 20 maybe even $50 but you're still playing with very small spends and your kind of growth is going to be therefore limited but it's also a safer place as well and most people will be expected to start out here but then the question is like where do you go next so if you're in the low risk area you're scaling really slowly if at all and now you're now you're super excited you think you've found a killer product or your categories are going really well and you now want to start to scale a lot faster what often happens and where a lot of people fall over is that they start scaling really quickly and they're not getting the reward they're expecting because they don't have the right controls in place and they're not using the right strategies to scale up and looking at the right metrics to know when also to scale down as well where I like to take things is up into the top left quadrant where you go into slow scaling comparatively but there's also higher reward and I'll talk about the scaling strategy and my preference for this especially when moving from low risk to high reward and then you've got the ultimate ultimate area that you want to play in which is the fast scale and high reward this is where you have the confidence in the product the audience the creative your bidding strategy your optimization your landing page your sales funnel everything is working well and now you're ready to scale a lot faster everyone wants to be here but you have to decide how you're going to get there you just can't go straight from low risk to high risk because you will unless

## [06:01 - 08:41]

you're an experienced Facebook marketer you will lose a lot of money high risk is where I play I know the controls that I need I'm able to scale really quickly I've been doing over many many years and I'm comfortable doing that it's not high risk for me because I have the controls in place but there's also the element of if you have that lower experience it will be high risk so going into where you want to be playing if you're moving up it's moving up into the low risk and high reward area and then if you're comfortable moving up into the high risk and high reward and so what I kind of try and define is start off in the beginner mode so beginner mode is you've got a spend that you're comfortable spending you're comfortable losing in terms of testing and getting the return back on validating products audiences creatives but then you move into the control of state if you are starting to scale out having a bit of control over what you're doing and learning as you go along will help you in the kind of times when things are not working so well when I see people move from beginner to high roller and they're expecting the big dollars to fall in that's when problems occur that's when your expectation doesn't match your experience and your ability to control your spend and that's where many other problems happen and I tell people to avoid this area in the bottom right where you're increasing your bid your budgets so quickly but you don't have the controls in place you don't have the structure the landing pages etc and you're just going to waste money so use the kind of controlled way of scaling up get to know your metrics get to know your account as well every account every site they'll react quite differently I know from managing multiple multiple e-coms stores and coaching and digital products etc etc no two accounts will be the same they'll always be nuances whether it's the creative or the product or the landing page or the sales process there's always something that will just have a slight difference which means even two very similar e-commerce businesses will react in different ways to performance as well and so even I will move especially with new accounts from beginner to the controller I want to learn and understand the pulse of the account as I go on and then I'm ready to move off into high roller I've got my controls in place I'm confident in my audiences my creatives my targeting my bidding etc and I'm able to scale up and part of this course is to teach you and support you in becoming a controller knowing your metrics
